title: IoT Device Template Schema
description: Comprehensive schema for IoT device templates with NuSMV validation constraints. NOTE: This schema serves as design documentation and offline validation reference. Runtime validation is enforced through DTO annotations and BoardStorageServiceImpl.validateTemplateManifestForNuSmv().

Name.description: Device template name (will be sanitized for NuSMV)

InitState.description: Initial state. For single-mode: 'stateName'. For multi-mode: 'mode1State;mode2State'. Empty string or '_' for wildcards allowed.

WorkingStates.description: All possible working states of the device

Name.description: State name. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated (e.g., 'auto;cool'). Spaces will be sanitized to underscores.

Dynamics.description: Variable changes in this state. Can be empty array if no changes. Each item must have either ChangeRate or Value.

ChangeRate.description: Integer change rate per time unit (mutually exclusive with Value)

Value.description: Direct value assignment (mutually exclusive with ChangeRate)

Trust.description: Trust level (will be normalized to lowercase)

Privacy.description: Privacy level (will be normalized to lowercase)

InternalVariables.description: Device internal variables and environment variables

Name.description: Variable name (must be valid NuSMV identifier)

IsInside.description: true = device-internal variable, false = environment variable (shared across devices)

PublicVisible.description: Whether variable is visible to external observers

Values.description: Enumerated values (mutually exclusive with LowerBound/UpperBound). Spaces in values will be sanitized.

LowerBound.description: Minimum value for integer range (requires UpperBound)

UpperBound.description: Maximum value for integer range. Attack mode scaling is handled automatically by SmvBoundsUtils.resolveEffectiveUpperBound().

NaturalChangeRate.description: Natural change rate: single value '1' or range '[-1, 1]'

ImpactedVariables.description: List of environment variables this device can affect

Transitions.description: Automatic state transitions triggered by conditions

Name.description: Transition name (will be sanitized for signal variables)

StartState.description: Starting state. Empty string '' means 'from any state'. '_' is wildcard. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated.

EndState.description: Target state (optional for internal variable transitions). For multi-mode: semicolon-separated. Partial changes like ';cool' allowed.

Attribute.description: Variable or mode name to check

Value.description: Value to compare against (spaces will be removed)

Signal.description: If true, creates a boolean signal variable for this transition

APIs.description: External APIs that can be called to control the device

Name.description: API name (will be sanitized for signal variables, appended with '_a')

StartState.description: Required starting state. Empty string '' means 'from any state' (common pattern). '_' is wildcard. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated.

EndState.description: Target state after API call. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated. Partial changes like ';cool' or 'auto;' allowed.

Trigger.description: Always null for APIs (triggers are for Transitions)

Assignments.description: Variable assignments when API is called

Signal.description: If true, creates a boolean signal variable '<name>_a' for this API

Contents.description: Content/data items managed by the device

$comment: Validation Notes: 1) Empty StartState ('') is intentional for APIs callable from any state. 2) Spaces in Mode/State names are removed (not replaced with underscores); other invalid characters replaced with underscores. 3) NuSMV reserved words will be escaped with '_' prefix. 4) Trust/Privacy values normalized to lowercase. 5) Multi-mode states use semicolon separator (e.g., 'mode1;mode2'). 6) Partial state changes use empty parts (e.g., ';cool' changes only mode2). 7) InternalVariable/ImpactedVariable names must not contain spaces (rejected at validation time). 8) InternalVariable and ImpactedVariable are allowed to have the same name (common pattern: device internal var affects environment var).
